problem: Tool re-execution on task retry has no idempotency guard — duplicate payments, emails, trades possible. When a CrewAI task fails and is retried — via max_retry_limit, exception handling, or external re-trigger — any @tool decorated function that already executed runs again. There's no mechanism to detect that a specific tool call already completed.
expected_solution_keywords: Tool re-execution on task retry has no idempotency guard — duplicate payments, emails, trades possible
memory_hint: Tool re-execution on task retry has no idempotency guard — duplicate payments, emails, trades possible. A retry of the same logical tool call should return the original result without re-executing the side effect.
failure_without_memory:
1. Create a CrewAI agent with a @tool that calls an external API (payment, email, etc.)
2. Have the tool execute successfully
3. Simulate a failure after tool execution but before the agent receives c
environment: Derive a stable request_id from tool arguments before execution, claim it in durable external storage, return the cached result on any retry with the same ID. The claim must happen outside the agent e
